Salesforce Note

Spoke with Jerrell (Manager) and Jeremy (Assistant Manager). They have access to Synchrony (BrakeMaster credit card), EasyPay, and SNAP, and primarily use us as their go-to option. Most conversations and usage are driven by approval rates. They do use SNAP, but it is typically consumer-driven (virtual card transactions initiated by the customer). Their POS does not update for these transactions, creating some risk and operational hassle since they cannot immediately confirm funding before releasing the vehicle; as a result, they try to avoid SNAP when possible.

Everything is in good standing and they had no questions. QR code is displayed. Team is very knowledgeable on credit, and this location is a strong area with high approval rates. Usage is inconsistent month to month, with some lower-volume periods, so this should be monitored to identify ...

Subject: 00UTO00000VnNL02AN
Full Comments: Laura from inside sales reached out to me yesterday requesting an onsite visit as this location was not able to access the BC and yesterday needed to send out an application but couldn't, told Laura I would stop by today. After I kept deleting EasyPay's cookies/cache it kept doing the same thing, I deleted them again and used my email to get to the correct login page. Then I used their email and got the verification code and was able to get this location into the NBC. From here, did some education and training on the NBC, dropped off pens, pads along with my information as the best POC when needed. Location should start using the BC now!

	•	The store is currently using Synchrony SNAP and U.S. Bank.
	•	They lead with Synchrony, which makes it critical for us to optimize the Synchrony waterfall.
	•	The waterfall is not currently turned on and needs to be activated as soon as possible.
	•	In the meantime, a QR code has been printed and displayed for customers who show interest.
	•	This is a slow season for the store.
	•	March–October is their busiest period.
	•	The customer base skews older, with many paying cash and using credit sparingly.

Key Takeaways / Next Steps:
	•	Priority action: Get the Synchrony waterfall turned on.
	•	Continue leveraging the QR code as a stopgap for interested customers.
	•	Strategy should account for:
	•	Older demographic
	•	Cash-first behavior
	•	Synchrony as the primary financing option
	•	Be positioned and fully optimized ahead of the March–October busy season.

Overall, very positive meeting with strong alignment and support for EasyPay.
